annotations.label: Linear map (additivity and homogeneity)

anchor.snippet: a linear map or linear function f ( x ) is a function that satisfies the two properties

annotations.note: `IsLinearMap` is defined by the two fields `map_add` and `map_smul`, matching the Wikipedia definition.

annotations.label: Additivity implies homogeneity for rationals

anchor.snippet: Additivity alone implies homogeneity for rational

annotations.note: Reinterprets any additive homomorphism between ℚ-modules as a ℚ-linear map, exactly encoding ℚ-homogeneity from additivity.

annotations.label: Additive continuous function is linear

anchor.snippet: The density of the rational numbers in the reals implies that any additive continuous function is homogeneous

annotations.note: `map_real_smul`/`AddMonoidHom.toRealLinearMap` proves a continuous additive map between real topological vector spaces is ℝ-linear via density of ℚ in ℝ.

annotations.label: Linear polynomial

anchor.snippet: a polynomial of degree 1 is said to be linear, because the graph of a function of that form is a straight line

annotations.note: Mathlib has no dedicated `IsLinear` predicate on polynomials, but `natDegree_eq_one` characterizes degree-1 polynomials as `C a * X + C b` with `a ≠ 0`.

annotations.label: Slope-intercept linear equation

anchor.snippet: a simple example of a linear equation is given by

annotations.note: The slope-intercept form `y = mx + b` is illustrative and not formalized as a named declaration in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Linear polynomial satisfies additivity/homogeneity iff constant term is zero

anchor.snippet: they do so if and only if the constant term

annotations.note: No Mathlib lemma characterizes when a degree-≤1 polynomial induces a linear (additive+homogeneous) map in terms of vanishing constant term.

annotations.label: Affine function

anchor.snippet: the function is called an affine function

annotations.note: `AffineMap` formalizes affine maps in full generality over affine spaces, subsuming the scalar case `x ↦ ax + b`.

annotations.label: Boolean linear function

anchor.snippet: In Boolean algebra , a linear function is a function

annotations.note: Mathlib has no dedicated notion of "linear Boolean function" as defined in Post's lattice / Boolean algebra context.

annotations.label: Linearity via truth table

anchor.snippet: A Boolean function is linear if one of the following holds for the function's truth table

annotations.note: No Mathlib declaration characterizes Boolean linearity via truth-table conditions.

annotations.label: Linear Boolean operations

anchor.snippet: Negation , Logical biconditional , exclusive or , tautology , and contradiction are linear functions

annotations.note: While xor/not/iff are defined in Mathlib, they are not classified as "linear Boolean functions" anywhere.

annotations.label: Linearity of homogeneous differential equations

anchor.snippet: Linearity of a homogeneous differential equation means that if two functions

annotations.note: Mathlib lacks a general statement that solutions of a homogeneous linear ODE form a vector space; no matching lemma was found under `Mathlib/Analysis/ODE`.
